# lai - the local AI programming environment

> lai plans, installs, and runs the best fully-local AI coding stack for the
> machine it is on (Windows / Linux / macOS; NVIDIA, AMD, Apple Silicon, or
> CPU-only). One stdlib-only Python package orchestrates llama.cpp +
> llama-swap, coding agents (Roo Code, Continue, Aider, OpenHands), repo RAG
> (Qdrant), a Vue dashboard, project scaffolding with a per-machine "gate",
> and optional explicit-use cloud fallbacks. All knowledge lives in an
> editable JSON catalog; everything that installs or downloads asks approval.

Key facts: entry point `lai.py` (CLI: `lai <command>`); implementation in
`laicore/` (core, stack, work, projects, webui, cli); recommendations in
`config/catalog.json` (models, hardware tiers, use cases, project stacks);
machine state in `state/` (gitignored); dashboard at http://127.0.0.1:8090;
OpenAI-compatible model endpoint at http://localhost:8083/v1 (configurable,
default 8080) serving models named `coder`, `thinker`, `vision`.

## Docs

- [LLM reference (read this first)](docs/llm-reference.md): complete
  machine-oriented reference - every command, file contract, HTTP API,
  concepts, and tutorial-ready workflows
- [README](README.md): human overview and quick start
- [Architecture & data flow](docs/01-architecture.md): design rules, winners
- [Model stack & memory budgets](docs/02-models.md): per-tier model choices,
  swap-cost policy
- [Agents & IDE setup](docs/03-agents-ide.md): Roo Code, Continue, Aider,
  OpenHands, Open WebUI configuration
- [Troubleshooting](docs/05-troubleshooting.md): OOM, slow generation,
  tool-call and indexing issues
- [Hardware catalog research](docs/06-hardware-catalog.md): why each tier
  recommends what it does, with sources
- [Projects & team workflow](docs/07-projects.md): lai new, .lai/ contract,
  lai gate, team vs personal config
- [Skills, web research, git AI](docs/08-skills-research-git.md)
- [Team server, tuning, docs RAG](docs/09-team-tune-docsrag.md)
- [Cloud fallback policy](docs/10-cloud-fallback.md)

## Optional

- [Contributing](CONTRIBUTING.md): logic-in-package / knowledge-in-data rule
- [VS Code extension](editors/vscode/README.md)
